title: Angolluma circes
taxonomy: Angolluma circes is classified as a species within the Apocynaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Angolluma circes among other plants like Frerea and Pachycereus, which are also part of the same family. More specifically, Angolluma circes is placed within the tribe Odonogonteae, which is a subfamily within the Apocynaceae family. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region, and its classification suggests that it shares certain characteristics with other plants in this family. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Angolluma circes within the plant kingdom.

intro: Angolluma circes is a succulent plant species native to the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Angolluma circes include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Angolluma circes include extracts and infusions, often used to treat arthritis, fever, and other inflammatory conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
